content.json:
# PureLog Stealer - Copyright Lure Multi-Stage Attack Hunting Workbook
---

**Threat Family:** PureLog Stealer (Information Stealer)
**Campaign Name:** Copyright Lures Mask a Multi-Stage PureLog Stealer Attack
**Reported:** March 19, 2026
**Source:** Trend Micro Research (Mohamed Fahmy, Allixon Kristoffer Francisco, Jonna Santos)
**Initial Access:** Phishing / Malvertising (Google Ads) - Copyright Violation Lure
**Delivery Method:** Malicious ZIP containing shellcode loader disguised as legal notice EXE
**Final Payload:** PureLog Stealer (fileless, in-memory via dual .NET loaders)

**Targeted Industries:**
- Healthcare
- Government
- Hospitality
- Education

**Targeted Countries:** Germany, Canada, United States, Australia

**Threat Intelligence Sources:**
- https://www.trendmicro.com/en_us/research/26/c/copyright-lures-mask-a-multistage-purelog-stealer-attack.html

---
> **Analyst Notes:** This campaign uses language-matched lures (German/English) delivered via phishing or Google Ads malvertising. The infection chain is fileless after initial execution - no final payload touches disk. Two parallel .NET loaders (Dgrfauysx.exe + Fsywsuac.exe) provide redundancy. AMSI is patched in memory before payload load. Registry persistence uses 'SystemSettings' as autorun key name.

## Attack Chain Summary

| Stage | Action | Key Indicator |
|-------|--------|---------------|
| 1 | User executes copyright lure EXE | `*Violation*Intellectual*Property*.exe` |
| 2 | Decoy PDF opened to distract user | `start "" ".\\document.pdf"` |
| 3 | Encrypted payload downloaded as invoice.pdf | `curl -A "curl/meow_meow"` |
| 4 | Decryption password fetched from C2 | `quickdocshare.com/DQ/key` |
| 5 | Renamed WinRAR (PNG extension) extracts payload | `FILE_*.png x -p"%i"` |
| 6 | Staging file deleted | `del invoice.pdf` |
| 7 | Working dir changed to fake Windows path | `C:\\Users\\Public\\Windows` |
| 8 | Renamed python.exe (svchost.exe) runs loader | `svchost.exe instructions.pdf` |
| 9 | AMSI patched in memory | AmsiScanBuffer patched via ctypes |
| 10 | Dual .NET loaders executed in memory | Dgrfauysx.exe + Fsywsuac.exe (ConfuserEx) |
| 11 | PureLog Stealer runs in managed heap | Credentials, wallets, screenshots exfiltrated |

content.title: Hunt 1 - File Hash IoC Match (ZIP, EXE, DLL, Renamed Python)
Hunt 2 - Network IoC Match (C2 Domains and IPs)

content.query:
// Hunt 3 - DNS IoC Match (C2 Infrastructure Domains)
// MITRE: T1568 - Dynamic Resolution
// MITRE: T1071.004 - Application Layer Protocol: DNS
DeviceEvents
| where Timestamp {timeframe}
| where ActionType == "DnsQueryResponse"
| extend DnsQuery = tostring(AdditionalFields.DnsQueryString)
| where DnsQuery has "quickdocshare.com"
    or DnsQuery has "bestshoppingday.com"
    or DnsQuery has "bestshopingday.com"
    or DnsQuery has "bestsaleshoppingday.com"
    or DnsQuery has "eideasrl.it"
| project-reorder Timestamp, DeviceName, DnsQuery, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessAccountName
| sort by Timestamp desc
content.title: Hunt 3 - DNS IoC Match (C2 Infrastructure Domains)
Hunt 4 - Copyright/IP Lure EXE Execution from Downloads
Hunt 5 - Suspicious curl Usage for Encrypted Payload + Key Retrieval

items.name: query - Behavioral Hunt 2 Curl Payload Download

content.title: Hunt 6 - Renamed WinRAR (PNG Extension) Extracting Password-Protected Archives
Hunt 7 - Renamed Python.exe (svchost.exe) Running PDF Loader from Public Path

items.name: query - Behavioral Hunt 4 Renamed Python Masquerade

content.query:
// Hunt 8 - Registry Run Key Persistence (SystemSettings Autorun Masquerade)
// MITRE: T1547.001 - Boot or Logon Autostart Execution: Registry Run Keys
DeviceRegistryEvents
| where Timestamp {timeframe}
| where RegistryKey has "\\Software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Run"
    and (
        RegistryValueName =~ "SystemSettings"
        or RegistryValueData has "Public\\Windows\\"
        or RegistryValueData has "instructions.pdf"
        or RegistryValueData has "svchost.exe"
    )
    and not (RegistryValueData startswith "C:\\Windows\\")
| project-reorder Timestamp, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName, RegistryKey, RegistryValueName, RegistryValueData, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine
| sort by Timestamp desc
content.title: Hunt 8 - Registry Run Key Persistence (SystemSettings Autorun Masquerade)
Hunt 9 - Silent AV Enumeration via PowerShell WMI SecurityCenter2
Hunt 10 - AMSI Bypass / Impairment from Non-Standard Process Paths
Hunt 11 - Python Runtime Files Dropped in Fake Windows Directory (C:\Users\Public\Windows)

items.name: query - Behavioral Hunt 8 Python Files Public Path

content.title: Hunt 12 - Chrome Launched with Sandbox-Disabled Flags by Suspicious Parent (Browser Credential Harvesting)

items.name: query - Behavioral Hunt 9 Chrome Credential Harvest

content.query:
// Hunt 13 - Full Kill Chain Correlation (Lure -> Download -> Masquerade -> AV Enum)
// MITRE: Full Kill Chain Correlation
let lure_exec = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp {timeframe}
| where FolderPath has "Downloads"
    and FileName endswith ".exe"
    and (
        ProcessCommandLine has "Copyright"
        or ProcessCommandLine has "Violation"
        or ProcessCommandLine has "Intellectual"
        or ProcessCommandLine has "Notice"
        or ProcessCommandLine has "Alleged"
        or FolderPath has "Copyright"
        or FolderPath has "Violation"
        or FolderPath has "Notice"
        or FolderPath has "Alleged"
    )
| project DeviceName, lure_time = Timestamp;
let curl_download = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp {timeframe}
| where FileName =~ "curl.exe"
    and (
        ProcessCommandLine has "meow_meow"
        or ProcessCommandLine has "invoice.pdf"
        or ProcessCommandLine has "/DQ"
    )
| project DeviceName, curl_time = Timestamp;
let svchost_masq = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp {timeframe}
| where FileName =~ "svchost.exe"
    and not (FolderPath startswith "C:\\Windows")
| project DeviceName, svchost_time = Timestamp;
let av_enum = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp {timeframe}
| where FileName =~ "powershell.exe"
    and ProcessCommandLine has "SecurityCenter2"
    and ProcessCommandLine has "AntivirusProduct"
| project DeviceName, avenum_time = Timestamp;
lure_exec
| join kind=inner curl_download on DeviceName
| join kind=inner svchost_masq on DeviceName
| join kind=inner av_enum on DeviceName
| where curl_time between (lure_time .. (lure_time + 30m))
    and svchost_time between (lure_time .. (lure_time + 60m))
    and avenum_time between (lure_time .. (lure_time + 90m))
| project DeviceName, lure_time, curl_time, svchost_time, avenum_time
| sort by lure_time desc
content.title: Hunt 13 - Full Kill Chain Correlation (Lure -> Download -> Masquerade -> AV Enum)

items.name: query - Behavioral Hunt 10 Kill Chain Correlation

content.title: Hunt 14 - CacheVersion=1337 Registry Marker (PureLog C2 Exfil Success Indicator)

items.name: query - Behavioral Hunt 11 Registry CacheVersion Marker

content.title: Hunt 15 - HTTPS C2 Exfiltration from Renamed Python Loader (Public Path svchost.exe)

content.json:
---
## Section 3: MITRE ATT&CK Technique Coverage

| Tactic | Technique ID | Technique Name | Hunt Query Coverage |
|--------|-------------|----------------|---------------------|
| Initial Access | T1566.002 | Phishing: Spearphishing Link | Hunt 1 (Behavioral) |
| Initial Access | T1566 | Phishing (Malvertising via Google Ads) | Hunt 2 (Behavioral) |
| Execution | T1204.002 | User Execution: Malicious File | Hunt 1 (IoC + Behavioral) |
| Execution | T1059.001 | PowerShell | Hunt 6 (Behavioral) |
| Execution | T1059.006 | Python | Hunt 4, 8 (Behavioral) |
| Persistence | T1547.001 | Registry Run Keys / Startup Folder | Hunt 5 (Behavioral) |
| Defense Evasion | T1036.003 | Masquerading: Rename System Utilities | Hunt 4 (Behavioral) |
| Defense Evasion | T1036.005 | Masquerading: Match Legitimate Name | Hunt 3 (Behavioral) |
| Defense Evasion | T1140 | Deobfuscate/Decode Files or Information | Hunt 3 (Behavioral) |
| Defense Evasion | T1562.001 | Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Tools (AMSI) | Hunt 7 (Behavioral) |
| Defense Evasion | T1564.001 | Hide Artifacts: Hidden Files and Directories | Hunt 8 (Behavioral) |
| Defense Evasion | T1027 | Obfuscated Files or Information (ConfuserEx, Base64, XOR) | Hunt 10 (Correlation) |
| Defense Evasion | T1620 | Reflective Code Loading (.NET Assembly.Load) | Hunt 4, 12 (Behavioral) |
| Discovery | T1518.001 | Security Software Discovery | Hunt 6 (Behavioral) |
| Discovery | T1082 | System Information Discovery (Machine Fingerprint) | Hunt 6, 10 (Behavioral) |
| Collection | T1113 | Screen Capture | Hunt 9, 12 (Behavioral) |
| Command and Control | T1071.001 | Application Layer Protocol: Web Protocols | Hunt 2 (IoC), Hunt 12 |
| Command and Control | T1105 | Ingress Tool Transfer | Hunt 2 (Behavioral) |
| Command and Control | T1568 | Dynamic Resolution | Hunt 3 (IoC) |
| Exfiltration | T1041 | Exfiltration Over C2 Channel | Hunt 12 (Behavioral) |
| Credential Access | T1555.003 | Credentials from Web Browsers | Hunt 9 (Behavioral) |

---
## Section 4: Investigation Playbook
### Step-by-Step Response Workflow

**Step 1 - Triage Initial Alert**
- Run IoC hunts (Hunts 1-3) to confirm presence of known-bad hashes, domains, or IPs
- Note the DeviceName and AccountName for scoping
- Check if DeviceName belongs to a targeted sector (Healthcare, Government, Hospitality, Education)

**Step 2 - Confirm Infection Chain**
- Run Behavioral Hunt 10 (Kill Chain Correlation) on the suspect device
- Verify presence of C:\\Users\\Public\\Windows\\ directory with Python DLL artifacts (Hunt 8)
- Check for svchost.exe in non-standard path (Hunt 4)

**Step 3 - Establish Persistence Footprint**
- Run Hunt 5 to check for SystemSettings registry autorun key
- Check HKCU\\Software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Run for suspicious entries
- Run Hunt 11 to check for CacheVersion=1337 marker (confirms successful C2 exfil already occurred)

**Step 4 - Assess Defense Evasion**
- Run Hunt 7 to confirm AMSI bypass activity
- Check if curl.exe was called with meow_meow user-agent (Hunt 2 Behavioral)
- Review any .png files that spawned extraction processes (Hunt 3)

**Step 5 - Scope C2 Communications**
- Run Hunt 12 to identify all outbound connections from the loader process
- Cross-reference IPs against known PureLog C2 (166.0.184.127, 64.40.154.96)
- Check for DNS queries to bestshopingday.com and quickdocshare.com (Hunt 3 IoC)

**Step 6 - Assess Data Exposure**
- Run Hunt 9 to identify Chrome browser sessions spawned by the loader
- Assume credentials, cookies, browser extensions, and screenshots are compromised
- Assume cryptocurrency wallet data may be at risk
- Notify affected user and initiate credential reset for all browser-stored passwords

**Step 7 - Containment**
- Isolate the affected device immediately
- Block all IoC domains and IPs at proxy/firewall
- Remove SystemSettings registry autorun key
- Delete C:\\Users\\Public\\Windows\\ directory and all contents

**Step 8 - Recovery**
- Re-image or deep clean the affected endpoint
- Force MFA challenge on all accounts accessible from the affected device
- Review email gateway for copyright-themed phishing messages delivered to the organization
- Check for Google Ads referral traffic to quickdocshare.com or eideasrl.it in proxy logs

---
## Section 5: Full IoC Reference

### File Hashes
| File | Hash Type | Hash Value |
|------|-----------|------------|
| Notice_ViolationIP_Rights.zip (lure archive) | SHA256 | 35efc4b75a1d70c38513b4dfe549da417aaa476bf7e9ebd00265aaa8c7295870 |
| Notice of Alleged Violation.exe / ADNotificationManager.exe | SHA256 | 1539dab6099d860add8330bf2a008a4b6dc05c71f7b4439aebf431e034e5b6ff |
| urlmon.dll (shellcode loader) | SHA256 | ac591adea9a2305f9be6ae430996afd9b7432116f381b638014a0886a99c6287 |
| svchost.exe (renamed python.exe) | SHA1 | f4532fc1e5d53a732fcc883f7125ceb06b985048 |

### Domains
| Domain | Role |
|--------|------|
| quickdocshare.com | Encrypted payload hosting + decryption key endpoint |
| dq.bestshoppingday.com | C2 redirect / alias |
| logs.bestshopingday.com | PureLog Stealer C2 (strongly associated) |
| mh.bestshopingday.com | C2 infrastructure |
| logs.bestsaleshoppingday.com | C2 infrastructure |
| cdn.eideasrl.it | Lure ZIP hosting |

### IP Addresses
| IP Address | Provider | Role |
|------------|----------|------|
| 166.0.184.127 | Unknown | PureLog Stealer primary C2 server |
| 64.40.154.96 | Tier.Net Technologies LLC | Secondary C2 / staging |
| 172.64.80.1 | Cloudflare | CDN (likely proxying C2 traffic) |

### File Paths and Names
| Path / Filename | Notes |
|----------------|-------|
| C:\\Users\\Public\\Windows\\ | Staging directory mimicking legitimate Windows path |
| C:\\Users\\Public\\Windows\\svchost.exe | Renamed python.exe loader |
| C:\\Users\\Public\\Windows\\instructions.pdf | Obfuscated Python loader script |
| .\\invoice.pdf | Encrypted payload archive (temporary) |
| .\\document.pdf | Decoy PDF |
| FILE_2025*_*.png | Renamed WinRAR executable |
| Dgrfauysx.exe | First in-memory .NET loader (ConfuserEx obfuscated) |
| Fsywsuac.exe | Second in-memory .NET loader (redundancy mechanism) |

### Registry Keys
| Key Path | Value Name | Value Data | Purpose |
|----------|-----------|------------|---------|
| HKCU\\Software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Run | SystemSettings | [path to svchost.exe] | Persistence autorun |
| HKCU\\...\\AppModel\\StateRepository | CacheVersion | 1337 | Anti-duplicate C2 exfil flag |
